finding: Interim re-decodes the ENTIRE accumulating buffer each tick under a fixed 30s INTERIM_WHISPER_TIMEOUT; a long recording whose full-clip decode exceeds 30s times out on late ticks, stalling captions when the buffer is largest.

forward_to_whisper maps a whisper failure/timeout to 500; on the interim path a slow-decode timeout is an EXPECTED transient condition, so it should surface as a transient status (503), not an Internal Server Error.

capability.streaming_enabled = enabled && settings.streaming_enabled omits runtime_ready && model_ready; a client gating solely on it could start the interim loop while the runtime/model is not ready.

No server-side interim rate/concurrency cap; stream_interval_ms is an advisory client hint. The validation comment 'Sub-300ms would flood the whisper instance' implies a protection that does not exist. Repeated full-buffer decodes on the single shared whisper-server can load-amplify.

Interim omits max_clip_seconds and there is no per-user rate/concurrency cap; each request re-decodes the whole growing buffer (~O(N^2) audio-seconds) on the single shared whisper-server, so multiple concurrent live-caption users (voice::transcribe held by all Users, live default ON) can saturate the instance and slow batch dictation. Byte-bounded per request, not in aggregate.
no defects found (no new permission; endpoint reuses VoiceTranscribe; frontend hides all new surfaces behind usePermission(VoiceTranscribe)).
no defects found (streaming endpoint faithfully mirrors transcribe.rs; store mirrors the supersession-token pattern).

Each interim tick rebuilds a Blob of ALL chunks, runs recordedBlobToWav16k on the whole accumulating clip (main thread), uploads the whole clip, and the server re-transcribes it: O(n^2) total client decode/encode + bandwidth + server CPU. For a 60s clip at 1s cadence ~30x the single final decode's work.

The live-caption strip renders the full stitched transcript with `truncate max-w-48` which ellipsizes the END; once the transcript exceeds the strip width the caption freezes showing the OPENING words and hides the newest speech for the rest of the recording.
no defects found (aria-hidden strip avoids aggressive announcement; toggle has aria-pressed + aria-label).

The toggle Tooltip is state-phrased ('Live captions on/off') while its aria-label is action-phrased ('Turn live captions off/on') — conflicting semantics on a toggle; make the tooltip action-phrased to match.

expect(voice.state.transcribeCount).toBe(1) is a non-polling snapshot after a wait that only checks voice-elapsed disappeared; status flips to 'transcribing' (removing voice-elapsed) BEFORE the async decode+POST, so the count can read 0 → flaky. Poll or wait on a completion UI signal.

Gold-smoke soft-skip only probes GitHub releases/latest; the base.en HF model download and host-triple asset are additional external gates not probed, so a reachable-GitHub-but-unreachable-HF env hard-fails instead of soft-skipping.
RE-AUDIT round (backend+tests): no new defects found after fixes
RE-AUDIT round (frontend): no new defects found after fixes

FB-1 clamp_wav_tail: unchecked u32 multiply byte_rate=sample_rate*block_align on user-controlled fmt fields → panic under overflow-checks (DoS) on a crafted WAV. FIXED via checked_mul + whole-clip fallback + unit test.
RE-AUDIT (FB-1 cap): clamp window/frame-align/no-op/header-rewrite correct; batch stays unclamped; 503 + can_transcribe gating intact
